opp.name: Seminole County_P2P_Opp - May '26
opp.next_step: Align with Gladys on what Seminole County wants to improve across purchasing, supplier access, and spend visibility. Review the current request to PO process and identify where approvals, controls, and user adoption need improvement. Understand how suppliers currently register, update information, share documents, and communicate with the county. Define the exact reporting views needed across spend, suppliers, categories, compliance, and department level activity. Run a tailored walkthrough showing how eProcurement, iSupplier,
opp.ais_why:
* Seminole County needs a structured procurement platform to "improve request handling, supplier access, and spend visibility" - a foundational public-sector procurement requirement.
* The driver is structuring the request-to-PO process and reporting, not AI capability.
* Next step is to "review current request to PO process and define reporting views"; Merlin and ML iAnalyze are in scope but AI is not part of the stated requirement. Qualified at 10%.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (discovery agenda, unexecuted, last modified 18 Jun)
Zero Avoma calls discovered (opp + account pull, 28 May–05 Jul)
No Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, or Value_to_Customer__c populated (SOQL Q3)
metrics.narrative: No business problem or quantified pain has been articulated by the buyer. Zero discovery calls were recorded or transcribed, leaving no buyer voice on cost reduction targets, cycle-time improvement, error rates, procurement spend under management, or ROI expectations. The Salesforce opportunity record carries no Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, or quantified metrics in any free-text field. Next_Step__c references a future discovery agenda (align on what to improve, review request-to-PO process, define reporting views) but that agenda was never executed—no follow-up call, no task completion, no field update. Without a single buyer conversation on record, the business case is completely unknown and this deal is uncoachable. The required move is to secure a live discovery call with Gladys and quantify the procurement pain Seminole County is solving for.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Gladys Marrozos, Decision Maker, Lead Procurement Administrator (Manager), no engagement date, no call history
Zero Avoma calls with Gladys or any Seminole County attendee (Avoma opp + account discovery 28 May–05 Jul)
No Tasks or Events indicating advocacy, introductions, or internal selling (Task query 28 May–05 Jul)
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed. Gladys Marrozos is the sole contact and is tagged Decision Maker, but zero engagement has occurred with her across the 38-day lifecycle—no calls, no emails on record, no task completions, no Avoma participation. There is no evidence of advocacy (no introductions, no internal documents shared, no agenda-pushing, no competitive defense) because there has been no interaction. Her access to the Economic Buyer or senior county leadership is unknown. Whether she is even aware this opportunity exists in Zycus's pipeline is unclear. The deal is completely single-threaded into a contact who has not engaged, and no other influencers, coaches, or executive sponsors are mapped. Without a champion who is actively selling internally and multi-threading access, this deal cannot progress even if re-engaged.

sources: No Competitors__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, or Replacing_What__c (SOQL Q3)
Zero Avoma calls with competitive discussion (Avoma discovery 28 May–05 Jul)
No Tasks referencing competitor names or competitive activity (Task query 28 May–05 Jul)
competition.narrative: Competitive landscape is completely unknown. The SF Competitors__c field is empty. No free-text fields (How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Why_not_Zycus__c) reference current vendors, incumbent solutions, or other P2P platforms being evaluated. Zero Avoma calls captured competitive mentions, and no tasks reference competitor activity. It is unknown whether Seminole County is running a multi-vendor RFP, replacing an incumbent (Ariba, Coupa, Oracle, Jaggaer), solving with internal tools, or sole-sourcing to the relevant stakeholder discovery intelligence, the competitive wedge and how-we-win strategy are undefined. The required move is to use the re-engagement call to uncover what Seminole County uses today, who else they are evaluating, and how Zycus differentiates on their specific pain points.

identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer. Zero discovery calls were recorded, so there is no buyer voice on what is broken in their current procurement process, what manual work or cycle-time delays they suffer, or what compliance/visibility gaps they need to close. The SF fields Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c, and What_if_this_is_not_done__c are all empty. Next_Step__c references a future discovery agenda to align on what Seminole County wants to improve and review the request-to-PO process for approval and control pain points, but that conversation never happened—no follow-up call, no task completion, no field update. The pain owner is also unmapped: Gladys (procurement manager) is the only contact, but whether she owns the pain or is a messenger for Finance, IT, or county leadership is unknown. Without confirmed pain and a pain owner, the deal has no validated business case.

sources: No Current_Contract_Expiration__c (SOQL Q2)
Zero Avoma calls discussing MSA, SOW, legal, InfoSec, or procurement mechanics (Avoma discovery 28 May–05 Jul)
No Tasks referencing contracting, legal review, or procurement workflow (Task query 28 May–05 Jul)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting and procurement mechanics are completely unknown. No MSA, SOW, legal review timeline, InfoSec requirements, or implementation partner involvement has been discussed on any call or documented in any Salesforce field. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is empty, so there is no forcing function from an expiring incumbent contract. Zero tasks reference legal review, redlining, or procurement channel. County government technology purchases often require formal procurement processes (RFP, vendor registration, insurance certificates, W-9, possibly Board approval), none of which are mapped. Without a single buyer conversation, the path from verbal agreement to signed contract is completely dark and the 13 Oct close timeline is unsupported by any known paper-process milestone.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Gladys Marrozos, Decision Maker, Lead Procurement Administrator (Manager), no engagement date

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged. Gladys Marrozos is tagged Decision Maker in the contact role but holds the title the deal owner's manager (Manager)—a procurement operations manager, not a CFO or budget authority for a $100k platform investment in county government. No calls, tasks, or free-text fields reference who controls the budget, who approves capital IT spend, or how procurement funding cycles work in Seminole County's fiscal structure. The SF fields Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Multiple_approvals__c, and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c are all empty. A county government P2P sale of this size typically requires cross-departmental sign-off (Finance/CFO, IT, possibly County Administrator or Board approval), none of whom are mapped. Without EB identification and engagement, budget authority is completely unknown and the deal cannot progress past discovery even if re-engaged.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer's decision process is completely unmapped. No calls, tasks, or free-text fields describe how Seminole County evaluates and selects P2P vendors, who participates at each stage, or what approval gates exist. The SF field What_is_the_decision_process__c is empty. Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c and Multiple_approvals__c are also empty, leaving the approval chain unknown. A county government procurement of this size typically follows a formal RFP or bid process with vendor shortlisting, evaluation committees, legal review, InfoSec sign-off, and potentially Board or County Administrator approval—none of which are documented. The call sequence itself (zero calls) provides no process intelligence. Without a discovery conversation, the path from interest to close is completely unknown and the 13 Oct close date has no grounding in the buyer's actual timeline or gates.

The product scope includes Merlin and ML iAnalyze, but whether Seminole County is AI Hungry, AI Curious, or AI Resistant is unmapped. Without discovery intelligence, AI positioning and Merlin value messaging are undefined.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Seminole County Government — Qualified · $100K · closes 13 Oct

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 0 reflects a weak position: momentum 46 vs stage-expected 50. Offsetting it: momentum 46 vs stage-expected 50.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 46 (roughly flat): Engagement cadence dropped (pulse dark). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (0), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 46, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

Without discovery intelligence, AI positioning and Merlin value messaging are undefined.

champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole (Gladys Marrozos, Decision Maker, zero engagement), zero Avoma calls, LastActivityDate null
champion_strength.summary: No champion exists. Gladys Marrozos is the sole contact and is tagged Decision Maker, but zero engagement has occurred—no calls, no emails, no tasks, no advocacy. There is no evidence she is aware of this opportunity, much less actively selling Zycus internally. The deal is single-threaded into a non-engaged contact, which is fatal.

items.action: RE-ENGAGE: Secure a live discovery call with Gladys Marrozos within 7 days to validate whether this opportunity is real. Agenda: confirm Seminole County's P2P procurement pain points and timeline, identify who controls the budget and who else needs to be in the room (Finance/CFO, IT leadership, County Administrator), map the decision process and approval gates, and assess competitive landscape (incumbent vendor, other solutions being evaluated). If Gladys does not respond or the opportunity is dead, mark Closed Lost and move on. Do not let this ghost deal occupy pipeline.
items.trigger: Zero buyer engagement across 38-day lifecycle, DARK engagement state, LastActivityDate null, no Avoma calls, no task completions, no field movement since 18 Jun.
items.expected_effect: Determines whether the deal is real. If Gladys engages, validates pain, timeline, budget authority, and process—foundational intelligence to assess viability. If no response, allows clean pipeline exit. Without this call the deal remains a placeholder with zero forward path.

items.action: MULTI-THREAD: If Gladys responds to rank-1 re-engagement, use that call to identify and secure introductions to the Economic Buyer (CFO, Finance Director, or budget authority), IT leadership (CIO or IT Director responsible for P2P platform architecture), and any other procurement stakeholders. Request a follow-up multi-party discovery session within 14 days of initial re-engagement to validate cross-functional alignment and decision authority.
items.trigger: Single-threaded into Gladys (procurement manager) with zero engagement, no EB/CFO/IT contacts mapped, no cross-functional validation for a $100k county government P2P sale.
items.expected_effect: Maps budget authority and cross-functional buying committee required for county government technology procurement. Reduces single-thread risk and creates visibility into who controls the decision and what approval gates exist. Without multi-threading, deal cannot progress past discovery.

items.action: VALIDATE TIMELINE: If re-engagement occurs, pressure-test the 13 Oct close date against Seminole County's actual procurement process and fiscal calendar. Ask Gladys: What is your procurement timeline? Do you plan to issue an RFP or sole-source? What are the evaluation, legal review, and Board approval gates? When does your fiscal year close and when must contracts be executed? Align the close date to the buyer's real timeline or reset to an honest forecast (likely Q1 2027 or later) and mark Pipeline or Omitted.
items.trigger: 13 Oct 2026 close date has no grounding in buyer timeline, process milestones, or fiscal calendar. County government P2P procurement cannot compress into 100 days with zero discovery, RFP, evaluation, or contracting progress.
items.expected_effect: Grounds the close date in buyer reality and resets forecast to honest category. Prevents false pipeline confidence and allows leadership to plan accurately. If buyer timeline is Q1 2027 or later, adjusting close date and forecast preserves deal integrity.

items.action: QUANTIFY THE BUSINESS CASE: During re-engagement discovery, extract and document the specific procurement pain Seminole County is solving for. Ask: What is broken in your current request-to-PO process? How much manual work, cycle-time delay, or maverick spend are you experiencing? What does non-compliance or lack of spend visibility cost you annually? What ROI or cost reduction do you need to justify this investment? Document quantified metrics (cycle time, error rates, spend under management, compliance %, FTE hours saved) in Customer_Business_Problem__c and Business_Objectives__c to build a defensible value case.
items.trigger: No business problem, pain, or quantified value case documented. Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c all empty. Zero buyer voice on what problem Seminole County is solving.
items.expected_effect: Creates a quantified business case that grounds the deal in real buyer pain and ROI expectations. Enables value-based positioning and validates whether Seminole County has urgency and budget justification to proceed. Without quantified pain, the deal is a feature evaluation with no compelling event.

items.action: ASSESS AI FIT AND POSITION MERLIN: During re-engagement discovery, assess Seminole County's AI appetite and Merlin relevance. Ask: Are you evaluating AI capabilities in your P2P platform selection? What procurement tasks would benefit from AI automation (supplier negotiation, spend forecasting, anomaly detection, contract intelligence)? Have you seen AI negotiation agents in action? If AI Curious or AI Hungry, position Merlin as the procurement AI differentiator—demo the Merlin negotiation agent on a relevant supplier scenario and tie it to their quantified savings goals. Document AI appetite in AIS_Why__c and request AIS scoring.
items.trigger: Products include Merlin and ML iAnalyze but AI fit is completely unvalidated. AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c all null. Zero buyer voice on AI needs or Merlin relevance.
items.expected_effect: Validates AI fit and positions Merlin as a competitive differentiator if Seminole County has AI appetite. If AI Hungry, Merlin becomes the wedge. If AI Resistant, deprioritizes Merlin in messaging and focuses on core eProcurement + iSupplier value. Without AI appetite assessment, Merlin is unpositioned and may confuse or dilute the pitch.

north_star_verdict.math: 100 days to close (13 Oct 2026). Created 28 May 2026, 38 days ago. Zero time-in-stage velocity: no progression markers, no submission dates, no field history movement beyond creation. No forward slip because nothing has moved. Deal sits in Qualified with no evidence of qualification activity—no discovery call content, no pain validation, no budget conversation, no multi-threading. A county government P2P procurement requiring discovery, RFP round, vendor shortlist, evaluation workshops, legal review, InfoSec, and contracting cannot compress into 100 days even with perfect execution, and this deal has not started execution. The close date is placeholder fiction with no grounding in buyer timeline or process milestones.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal created 38 days ago with zero recorded buyer engagement across entire lifecycle. 100 days to close with no discovery, no MEDDPICC validation, and complete silence since creation. This is not a stalled deal—this is a non-existent deal that never started.

flags: RE-ENGAGE IMMEDIATELY: 29 consecutive days of buyer silence on a 29-day-old deal means the relationship never started. Grace (owner) must re-contact Gladys Marrozos within 48 hours to restart the discovery conversation outlined in NextStep and validate interest. If no reply in 5 business days, escalate to Michael McCarthy (owner's manager) for an executive-to-procurement-leadership bridge (Gladys reports into Bob Hunter, Procurement Supervisor, or Lorie Bailey-brown, CFO).

MULTI-THREAD BEFORE QUALIFYING FURTHER: One contact (Gladys) with zero engagement is a ghost-risk setup. Discovery must immediately expand to IT leadership (Reginald Hardee, Jeff Leveroni for ERP integration and Merlin AI strategy), senior procurement (Bob Hunter), and finance (Lorie Bailey-brown, CFO, for budget authority and ROI validation). A county P2P deal requires cross-functional buy-in; single-threading guarantees failure.

RECORD EVERY BUYER CONVERSATION: The 28 May introductory call was not recorded, leaving zero discovery intelligence (pain, competitive landscape, decision process, stakeholders, timeline). Every future call with Seminole County must be recorded and transcribed to capture buyer requirements, validate fit, and build a defensible sales strategy. Without call content this deal is uncoachable and unforecastable.

best_practice_check.summary: This deal violates every fundamental enterprise sales discipline. It was created 38 days ago with zero buyer engagement, no discovery, no MEDDPICC validation, no multi-threading, and no forward movement. It sits in Qualified with a 13 Oct close date that has no grounding in buyer timeline, process, or milestones. The engagement state is DARK: no LastActivityDate, no Avoma calls, no task completions, no field updates beyond creation. This is not a stalled deal—this is a placeholder opportunity that never started and may never have been real.

competitive_position.summary: Competitive landscape completely unknown. No incumbent vendor identified, no alternative solutions discussed, no competitive RFP context documented. Zero discovery intelligence on what Seminole County uses today or who else they are evaluating. Without this baseline, competitive positioning and differentiation strategy are undefined.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is completely unvalidated. Products include Merlin (AI negotiation agent), ML iAnalyze (AI spend analytics), plus eProcurement and iSupplier, but no discovery call assessed whether Seminole County has AI appetite, what AI use cases matter to them, or how Merlin fits their procurement workflow. AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, and AIS_Why__c are all null. Without buyer voice on AI needs, Merlin is a product-scope placeholder with no validated fit or positioning strategy.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Customer expectations are completely unmapped. No discovery call captured what Seminole County expects from a P2P platform, what their evaluation criteria are, or how eProcurement + iSupplier + iAnalyze + Merlin align to their stated priorities. The product scope (eProcurement, iSupplier, ML iAnalyze, Merlin) was set at opportunity creation but has never been validated against buyer requirements because no requirements conversation occurred. Fit is unvalidated.

hard.ais_why:
* Seminole County needs a structured procurement platform to "improve request handling, supplier access, and spend visibility" - a foundational public-sector procurement requirement.
* The driver is structuring the request-to-PO process and reporting, not AI capability.
* Next step is to "review current request to PO process and define reporting views"; Merlin and ML iAnalyze are in scope but AI is not part of the stated requirement. Qualified at 10%.
hard.next_step: Align with Gladys on what Seminole County wants to improve across purchasing, supplier access, and spend visibility.Review the current request to PO process and identify where approvals, controls, and user adoption need improvement.Understand how suppliers currently register, update information, share documents, and communicate with the county.Define the exact reporting views needed across spend, suppliers, categories, compliance, and department level activity.Run a tailored walkthrough showing how eProcurement, iSupplier,
